summary: Phase 10 (Portfolio Gallery + Email service) tested end-to-end. Backend: 15/15 pytest portfolio + email-console tests PASS. Email service console fallback verified via supervisor logs (tpl_welcome on register, tpl_design_phase_quote on phase creation – all visible as 'EMAIL/CONSOLE' lines, no errors). All portfolio CRUD endpoints work: public GET (no auth), specialist own GET, POST with URL/base64, 4MB cap rejection, ownership-scoped PUT/DELETE returning 404 cross-user. Seed is idempotent (3 items present after restarts + 1 added in prior iteration = 4 total across spec1+spec2). Frontend: SpecialistDashboard shows 'Portofoliu' button → opens 'Portofoliul meu' modal with 3 items + 'Adaugă proiect' editor (title/desc/categorie/stil/locație/suprafață/imagine cover fields and gallery). Public profile /specialists/{id} renders 'Portofoliu de proiecte' section above 'Recenzii recente' with cover, style badge, category, location, surface chips. Click → lightbox with prev/next chevrons and close X (data-testid: portfolio-lightbox, lightbox-close, lightbox-prev, lightbox-next). Regression PASS: Client dashboard (Interior Design card, Digital Twin, faze design, chat), Admin tabs (Sumar/Analytics/Specialiști/Dispute) all functional.

issues: Lightbox title text 'Sistem HVAC Premium - Vila Pipera' overlaps slightly with the background specialist header card ('Mihai Ionescu') because backdrop appears semi-transparent / low opacity. Minor – consider darker backdrop or higher contrast title bar.

action_items: Optional: Increase lightbox backdrop opacity in PortfolioGallery (SpecialistProfile) for better contrast – title currently bleeds into the background header card.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py PortfolioItemIn allows arbitrary gallery list size – no explicit length cap in model (frontend says up to 12 only). Consider adding a Pydantic max_items=12 constraint or server-side trim for defense-in-depth.

MAX_IMAGE_SIZE_BYTES is 5_500_000 with comment '~4MB base64' – this is correct (base64 ratio 4/3) but a misnamed const could confuse future devs; consider renaming or splitting per-image vs total payload limits.
Portfolio endpoints rely on bson ObjectId – consistent with existing pattern. id is returned as str via serialize_doc, _id stripped. Good.

email_service.py uses asyncio.create_task fire-and-forget – on prod with real provider, consider logging failures to a dead-letter queue or at least via try/except inside the task so a Resend outage doesn't silently lose emails.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows (portfolio UI, lightbox, admin tabs, client dashboard)

test_credentials: specialist@propmanage.io / Spec123!, specialist2@propmanage.io / Spec123!, client@propmanage.io / Client123!, admin@propmanage.io / Admin123! (read from review request and /app/memory/test_credentials.md not consulted as creds were explicit)
seed_data_creation: No new seed; tests are self-cleaning (TEST_ prefix portfolio items created + deleted within each test).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 10 portfolio + email infrastructure is solid. Next iteration could exercise: (1) actual dispute open/resolve emails (the regression test only confirms register + phase quote console logs – not all 6 templates were triggered live). (2) Real Resend send path once RESEND_API_KEY is set in backend/.env. (3) Portfolio gallery size cap (verify 12-image limit is enforced – currently frontend-only; server accepts unbounded gallery list). (4) Edge case: deleting a portfolio item then checking it disappears from public /specialists/{id}/portfolio.
rca of the issue: N/A – no bugs found this iteration.
